pages.title: Free QR Code Generator with Logo — No Sign-Up
pages.meta: Create a free QR code with your logo, custom colors and styled dots. No sign-up, no expiry. Download PNG or SVG — generated right in your browser.
pages.h1: Free QR Code Generator with Logo
pages.subhead: Design a custom QR code in seconds — add your logo, pick your colors, download PNG or SVG. No account, no watermark, no expiry.

tool.note: Full generator: content field + style controls (dots, finders, colors, size, error-correction, logo upload) + live preview + Download PNG/SVG.

pages.intro: QR Code Agent is a free QR code generator that runs entirely in your browser. Paste any link, choose a dot style and colors, drop in your logo, and download a high-resolution PNG or print-ready SVG. There is no sign-up, no watermark, and the codes never expire because the data lives inside the image itself.

howto.title: How to make a QR code

steps.name: Enter your content
steps.text: Paste the URL or text you want the QR code to open.

steps.name: Style it
steps.text: Pick dot and finder shapes, set your colors, and add a center logo.

steps.name: Download
steps.text: Export a PNG for screens or an SVG for print, then share or print it.

benefits.t: Place a logo in the center with a circle or square frame. Q/H error correction keeps it scannable.
Star, diamond, circle or square dots and custom finder patterns — not the usual plain black grid.
Vector export scales to any size, from a business card to a billboard, with no blur.
Everything happens on your device. Your links and logos are never uploaded to a server.

faq.q: Is this QR code generator really free?
faq.a: Yes. Every feature is free — no account, no watermark, and no limit on how many codes you make or download.

faq.q: Do the QR codes expire?
faq.a: No. The codes are static, so the data is encoded directly in the image and works forever. There is nothing to renew.

faq.q: Can I add a logo?
faq.a: Yes. Upload a PNG logo and it is placed in the center. Use Quartile (Q) or High (H) error correction so it still scans reliably.

faq.q: PNG or SVG — which should I download?
faq.a: Use PNG for screens and social; use SVG for anything printed, since it scales to any size without losing quality.

pages.title: QR Code Generator with Logo — Free, No Sign-Up
pages.meta: Add your logo to a QR code for free. Upload a PNG, keep it scannable with high error correction, and download PNG or SVG. No account needed.
pages.h1: Add Your Logo to a QR Code — Free
pages.subhead: Drop your logo into the center of a fully styled QR code and download it in seconds. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.

tool.note: Generator with the logo-upload panel emphasized (logo drop zone, shape circle/square, border toggle, error-correction hint).

pages.intro: A logo in the center makes a QR code instantly recognizable and on-brand. QR Code Agent lets you upload a transparent PNG, frame it as a circle or square, and keep the code scannable by nudging error correction to Q or H. Style the dots and colors to match your brand, then export a crisp PNG or a print-ready SVG — all free and all in your browser.

howto.title: How to add a logo to a QR code

steps.name: Enter your link
steps.text: Paste the URL the QR code should open.

steps.name: Upload your logo
steps.text: Drop in a PNG (transparent background works best) and pick a circle or square frame.

steps.name: Set error correction to Q or H
steps.text: Higher recovery keeps the code readable with a logo covering the center, then download.

benefits.t: Built-in guidance sets error correction high enough that the logo never breaks the scan.
Match the logo shape to your brand, with an optional border for contrast.
Combine the logo with custom foreground/background colors and styled dots.
Download SVG so the logo and code stay sharp at any print size.

faq.q: Will a logo stop the QR code from scanning?
faq.a: Not if error correction is high enough. Use Q (25%) or H (30%) and keep the logo to roughly the center third of the code.

faq.q: What logo file works best?
faq.a: A PNG with a transparent background gives the cleanest result. High-contrast, simple marks scan most reliably.

faq.q: Is adding a logo free?
faq.a: Yes. Logo upload, styling, and PNG/SVG download are all free with no account.

pages.title: WiFi QR Code Generator — Free, Instant Connect
pages.meta: Make a free WiFi QR code. Guests scan to join your network — no typing the password. Style it, add a logo, download PNG or SVG. No sign-up.
pages.subhead: Let guests join your WiFi with one scan — no more reading passwords aloud. Free, styled, and downloadable as PNG or SVG.

tool.note: TYPE-SPECIFIC BUILD: fields = Network name (SSID), Encryption (WPA/WPA2 · WEP · None), Password, Hidden network toggle → encodes WIFI: payload. Then the standard style + download controls.

pages.intro: A WiFi QR code lets anyone connect to your network by scanning it with their phone camera — no typing a long password. Enter your network name, security type, and password, style the code to match your space, and print it on a table tent, window, or welcome card. Everything is generated on your device, so your WiFi password is never sent anywhere.

howto.title: How to create a WiFi QR code

steps.name: Enter your WiFi details
steps.text: Type your network name (SSID), choose the encryption type, and enter the password.

steps.name: Style and brand it
steps.text: Pick colors and dot style, and optionally add your logo.

steps.name: Download and display
steps.text: Export PNG or SVG and print it where guests can scan it.

benefits.t: Guests join instantly on iOS and Android — no password typing, no errors.
The code is built in your browser; your credentials are never uploaded.
SVG export prints crisply on table tents, signage, or a framed card.
Colors, dot styles, and a logo make it look intentional, not generic.

faq.q: Is the WiFi QR code free?
faq.a: Yes — generate, style, and download as many WiFi QR codes as you like, with no account.

faq.q: Does it work on iPhone and Android?
faq.a: Yes. Both scan WiFi QR codes with the built-in camera and prompt to join the network.

faq.q: Is my WiFi password safe?
faq.a: Yes. The code is generated entirely in your browser — the password is never sent to a server.

faq.q: Which encryption type should I pick?
faq.a: Most modern routers use WPA/WPA2. Choose 'None' only for open networks.

pages.intro: A vCard QR code holds your full contact details, so a single scan saves your name, phone, email, and company straight into someone's phone. It is the fastest way to share contact info at events, on a business card, or in an email signature. Fill in your details, brand the code with your colors and logo, and download it — no app and no account required.

howto.title: How to make a vCard QR code

steps.name: Enter your contact details
steps.text: Add your name, phone, email, company, and anything else you want saved.

steps.name: Brand the code
steps.text: Set colors, dot style, and add your logo for a professional look.

steps.name: Download and share
steps.text: Put the PNG in your email signature or the SVG on a printed business card.

benefits.t: All your details drop into the phone's contacts app — no manual typing.
SVG export keeps the code sharp on business cards and badges.
Regenerate anytime your details change and reprint — it's free.
A branded contact code looks far more credible than a plain one.

faq.q: What can a vCard QR code store?
faq.a: Name, phone number(s), email, company, job title, website, and address — the standard vCard fields.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes. Create and download unlimited vCard QR codes with no sign-up or watermark.

faq.q: Can I edit it later?
faq.a: These are static codes, so to change details you regenerate and reprint. That also means they never expire.

pages.intro: Adding a QR code to a business card bridges paper and phone: a quick scan can save your full contact details as a vCard, open your portfolio, or jump to a booking page. Choose what the code should do, brand it with your colors and logo, and export a print-ready SVG that stays crisp at business-card size. It is free, with no account and no watermark.

howto.title: How to add a QR code to a business card

steps.name: Choose the destination
steps.text: Save-my-contact (vCard) or a link to your site, portfolio, or booking page.

steps.name: Brand and size it
steps.text: Match your card's colors, add your logo, and keep the code at least 2 cm wide.

steps.name: Download SVG for print
steps.text: Hand the vector file to your printer so the code stays sharp.

benefits.t: Pair with a vCard so people store your details instantly — no typos.
SVG plus a quiet-zone reminder keeps small printed codes scannable.

No subscription and no expiry — reprint whenever you refresh your cards.

faq.q: What should the QR code on a business card link to?
faq.a: Most people use a vCard (save contact) or a link to a portfolio, LinkedIn, or booking page. Pick whichever drives your goal.

faq.q: How small can the code be?
faq.a: Keep it about 2 cm (0.8 in) or larger with a clear quiet zone around it so phone cameras lock on.

faq.q: Is it free to use on printed cards?
faq.a: Yes. There is no license fee, watermark, or expiry — download the SVG and print.

Ask for the review at the happiest moment — right after a good meal.
Print the review or reorder link on the bag or box lid.

faq.q: Is it free for my restaurant?

howto.title: How to make a custom QR code

steps.name: Enter your link or text
steps.text: Type what the code should open when scanned.

Restyle the three corner markers as square, rounded, or circular.
Set foreground and background, then drop a logo in the center.
No sign-up, no watermark, and the code never expires.

faq.q: Does styling break the scan?
faq.a: Not if you keep contrast high and error correction at Q or H when a logo is present. Dark dots on a light background scan most reliably.

faq.q: Can I use my brand colors?
faq.a: Yes. Set any foreground and background color. Keep them clearly different so scanners can read the pattern.

faq.q: Is it really free?
faq.a: Yes. Every style option and both download formats are free, with no account and no watermark.

pages.meta: Turn a wallet address or payment URI into a QR code, free. Works for Bitcoin and other crypto. Brand it and download PNG or SVG. No account.
pages.subhead: Encode a wallet address or payment URI so people can scan instead of typing it. Free and branded.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste your wallet address or a payment URI such as bitcoin:address?amount=0.01'. Tell the user to copy the address exactly and test the code with their own wallet app before sharing.

pages.intro: Wallet addresses are long strings that are easy to mistype. A QR code lets someone scan the address you already have straight into their wallet app. Paste the address or a full payment URI, style the code, and download it free. Nothing is uploaded, everything is generated in your browser, and the code never expires.

howto.title: How to make a crypto QR code

steps.name: Copy your address
steps.text: Take the receiving address or payment URI from your own wallet.

steps.name: Paste and style it
steps.text: Add the string, set colors, and add a logo if you want.

steps.name: Test, then download
steps.text: Scan it with a wallet app to confirm the address matches, then export PNG or SVG.

benefits.t: Scanning removes the risk of a wrong character in a long address.
Encode a full payment URI if you want the amount prefilled in the wallet app.
The code is generated locally, so your address is never sent to a server.
No account, no watermark, no expiry.

faq.q: Which coins does it work with?
faq.a: Any of them. The code stores whatever text you paste, so a Bitcoin, Ethereum, or other wallet address all work the same way.

faq.q: Should I test it first?
faq.a: Yes. Always scan your own code with a wallet app and check the address character for character before you print or publish it.

faq.q: Do you store my address?
faq.a: No. The code is generated in your browser and nothing is uploaded or tracked.

pages.subhead: Paste a link, get a scannable code. Free, no sign-up, never expires.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste any link, including https://'. Standard style + download. Keep this page tool-first with minimal copy.

pages.intro: Paste any web address and get a QR code that opens it. Works for landing pages, forms, docs, booking links, anything with a URL. Style the dots and colors, add a logo, and download a PNG or SVG. Free, no sign-up, and the link is baked into the image so it never expires.

howto.title: How to make a URL QR code

steps.name: Paste your link
steps.text: Include https:// so scanners open it directly.

steps.name: Style it
steps.text: Set colors and dot style, and add a logo.

steps.name: Download
steps.text: Export PNG or SVG.

benefits.t: Landing pages, forms, docs, booking links, app listings.
SVG stays sharp from a business card to a billboard.
Colors, dot styles, and a logo in the center.
No account and no expiry.

faq.q: Does the code expire?
faq.a: No. The link is stored in the image itself, so it keeps working as long as the page it points to is online.

faq.q: Can I change the link later?
faq.a: No. These are static codes, so the URL is fixed once you download. Point it at a page you control if the destination may change.

pages.title: App Store QR Code Generator — Free
pages.meta: Make a free QR code for your app listing. Scan to open the App Store or Google Play page. Brand it and download PNG or SVG. No sign-up.
pages.subhead: One code that sends people straight to your app listing. Free, branded, print-ready.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste your App Store, Google Play, or smart-link URL'. Mention that a smart link covering both stores works too. Standard style + download.

pages.intro: Asking people to search a store by name loses installs. A QR code drops them on your listing in one scan. Paste your App Store or Google Play URL, or a smart link that routes each phone to the right store, then brand the code and download it. Free, no sign-up, and it never expires.

howto.title: How to make an app download QR code

steps.name: Copy your listing link
steps.text: Grab the App Store, Google Play, or smart-link URL.

steps.name: Style it
steps.text: Set colors and dot style, and add your app icon as the logo.

steps.name: Download
steps.text: Export PNG for screens or SVG for print.

benefits.t: The scan opens your listing directly, so nobody installs the wrong app.
Point it at a smart link and each phone lands on iOS or Android.
Drop the icon in the center so the code is recognizable at a glance.
No account, no watermark, no expiry.

faq.q: Can one code cover iOS and Android?
faq.a: Yes, if you point it at a smart link or a landing page that detects the device and forwards to the right store.

faq.q: Where should I put it?
faq.a: Packaging, posters, receipts, trade-show stands, and slide decks all work. Keep it at least two centimetres wide in print.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes, with no account or watermark.

pages.title: Event QR Code Generator — Free, No Sign-Up
pages.meta: Create a free event QR code for tickets, check-in, or your event page. Brand it and download PNG or SVG. No account and no expiry.
pages.subhead: Link to tickets, a check-in page, or the event details in one scan. Free and branded.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste your ticket, registration, or event page link'. Standard style + download.

pages.intro: An event QR code points at whatever attendees need next: the ticket page, the registration form, the check-in link, or the agenda. Put it on invites, badges, posters, and signage so people get there in one scan. Paste the link, brand the code, and download it free with no sign-up.

howto.title: How to make an event QR code

steps.name: Pick the destination
steps.text: Copy the link to your ticket, registration, or event page.

steps.name: Brand it
steps.text: Set colors to match the event and add your logo.

steps.name: Download and print
steps.text: Export SVG for signage and PNG for email or social.

benefits.t: Send people straight to the page that sells or confirms a spot.
Put the code on badges and door signage so staff scan instead of typing.
One code on the invite can open the agenda, venue map, or directions.
No account and no expiry, so codes printed early still work on the day.

faq.q: Can it hold the ticket itself?
faq.a: No. A QR code stores a link, not a file, so point it at your ticketing or registration page.

faq.q: How big should it print?
faq.a: At least two centimetres wide for handouts, and much larger for signage people scan from a few metres away.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes, with no sign-up, no watermark, and no expiry.

pages.title: Phone Number QR Code Generator — Free
pages.meta: Make a free QR code for a phone number. One scan starts the call — no typing digits. Brand it, add a logo, download PNG or SVG. No sign-up.
pages.subhead: Scan to call — the number opens in the dialer, ready to ring. Free and branded.

tool.note: URL mode encoding a tel: link. Helper: 'enter your number in international format, e.g. +1 415 555 0123' and note that the code becomes tel:+14155550123. Standard style + download.

pages.intro: A phone QR code puts your number into the dialer with one scan, so nobody mistypes it off a flyer or a van door. Enter the number in international format, brand the code, and download it free — no sign-up, and it never expires.

steps.name: Enter the number
steps.text: Use international format, like +14155550123.

steps.name: Style it
steps.text: Add colors and your logo.

steps.name: Download
steps.text: Export PNG or SVG.

benefits.t: The number lands in the dialer — no typing, no typos.
SVG stays sharp on cards, signs, and vehicle decals.
Colors and a logo keep it on-brand.
No account, no expiry.

faq.q: Does scanning place the call automatically?
faq.a: No. The phone opens the dialer with your number filled in and the person taps to call.

faq.q: What number format should I use?
faq.a: International format with the country code, like +14155550123. It works for scanners anywhere.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes, with no sign-up or watermark.

pages.title: SMS QR Code Generator — Free, No Sign-Up
pages.meta: Make a free SMS QR code. One scan opens a text to your number with the message prefilled. Brand it, add a logo, download PNG or SVG. No sign-up.
pages.subhead: Scan to text — your number and message are already filled in. Free and branded.

tool.note: URL mode encoding an sms: link. Helper: number in international format plus an optional prefilled message → sms:+14155550123?body=JOIN. Standard style + download.

pages.intro: An SMS QR code opens a new text to your number with the message already written, so people just hit send. Useful for keyword opt-ins, support requests, and table service. Enter the number and message, brand the code, and download it free.

howto.title: How to make an SMS QR code

steps.name: Enter the number
steps.text: Use international format, like +14155550123.

steps.name: Add a message
steps.text: Optional — prefill a keyword like JOIN or a support prompt.

steps.name: Style and download
steps.text: Add colors and a logo, then export PNG or SVG.

benefits.t: Set the keyword or question so the sender only taps send.
Print it where people already stand — counters, tables, packaging.
Colors and a logo keep it on-brand.
No account, no expiry.

faq.q: Does it send the text automatically?
faq.a: No. It opens the messaging app with the number and message filled in, and the person taps send.

faq.q: Do all phones support a prefilled message?
faq.a: Most modern iPhones and Android phones do. If one ignores the message body, the number still fills in correctly.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes, with no sign-up or watermark.

pages.title: Text QR Code Generator — Free
pages.meta: Make a free text QR code. Scan to show a plain-text message — no link, no internet needed. Brand it and download PNG or SVG. No sign-up.
pages.subhead: Show a plain-text message on scan — no link, no connection required. Free and branded.

tool.note: URL mode with plain text accepted as-is (no URL required). Helper: 'type any text — it is shown on the scanner screen'. Note that shorter text keeps the code easier to scan. Standard style + download.

pages.intro: A text QR code stores words instead of a link, so the message appears on screen the moment someone scans it. Handy for serial numbers, instructions, shelf notes, and event details. Type the text, brand the code, and download it free.

howto.title: How to make a text QR code

steps.name: Type your text
steps.text: Keep it short so the code stays easy to scan.

steps.name: Style it
steps.text: Add colors and your logo.

steps.name: Download
steps.text: Export PNG or SVG.

benefits.t: The text is in the image, so no internet is needed to read it.
Nothing to upload or maintain — just the words you typed.
Colors and a logo keep it on-brand.
No account, no expiry.

faq.q: How much text can it hold?
faq.a: A few hundred characters is comfortable. More text makes the pattern denser and harder to scan, so keep it brief.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes, with no sign-up or watermark.

pages.title: QR Codes for Retail — Free Generator, No Sign-Up
pages.meta: Free QR codes for retail — shelf labels, product info, loyalty sign-up and reviews. Add your logo, download PNG or SVG. No account, no expiry.
pages.intro: A shelf label only has room for a price. A QR code next to it can hold the size guide, the ingredients, the how-to video, or the loyalty sign-up form. Here are the codes retail stores actually use on shelves, receipts and windows, each with a free tool to make it.

tool.note: Full generator embedded so store teams can make any code on the ICP page itself.

tools.name: Product info sheet
tools.text: Link a hosted spec sheet, size guide or care instructions from a shelf label or swing tag.

tools.name: Get reviews
tools.text: Put a review link on the receipt or at the till while the purchase is still fresh.

tools.name: Loyalty & social
tools.text: Send shoppers to your sign-up page or Instagram from the window or the bag.

scenarios.t: One small code per line gives a shopper the full spec without a staff member.
Sizing, materials and care details that never fit on the tag itself.
A code on the counter beats reading an email address out loud.
Ask for a review or a reorder link right after a good purchase.
Let passers-by browse the range or shop online when you're closed.

faq.q: Is it free for my store?
faq.a: Yes — every tool here is free, with no sign-up and no expiry. Print as many shelf labels as you need.

faq.q: Can I change what a shelf code points to?
faq.a: Only if it points to a page you control. Update that page and the printed code keeps working; change the link itself and you need a new code.

faq.q: How small can a shelf code be?
faq.a: Around 2 cm square scans reliably at arm's length. Download the SVG so it stays sharp at small sizes.

pages.intro: A yard sign gets seen by people who will never call the number on it. A QR code lets them scan from the sidewalk and see photos, the floor plan, or the video tour on the spot. Here are the codes agents use on signs, sheets and cards, each with a free tool to make it.

tool.note: Full generator embedded so agents can make any code on the ICP page itself.

tools.name: Listing sheet
tools.text: Link a hosted listing PDF or floor plan from the yard sign, the window card or the flyer.

tools.name: Agent contact card
tools.text: One scan saves your name, phone, email and agency to a buyer's phone.

tools.name: Virtual tour
tools.text: Send viewers straight to the walkthrough video or 3D tour instead of a phone number.

scenarios.t: Passers-by scan from the sidewalk and see the full listing without calling.
Print one code that opens the photo gallery, the floor plan or the PDF.
Point a code at the walkthrough video so a drive-by turns into a viewing.
A code on the door frame opens your visitor form — no clipboard, no queue.
A vCard code on your card or sign saves your details in one scan.

faq.q: Is it free for agents?
faq.a: Yes — no sign-up, no watermark, and the codes never expire. Make one per listing if you want.

faq.q: What happens when the property sells?
faq.a: If the code points to a listing page you control, update that page to a sold notice or your other listings. The printed code keeps working.

faq.q: Will it scan from a yard sign?
faq.a: Yes, if you print it large enough — roughly 8 cm square for sidewalk distance. Use the SVG so it stays crisp at sign size.

pages.intro: Running an event means printing things: badges, signage, programs, lanyards. A QR code on each one saves the attendee from typing anything. This is the organizer's hub — the codes worth printing before doors open, and the free tool for each. For a single ticket or invite code, start on the event QR code page.

tool.note: Full generator embedded on the ICP page. Hub only — must not target 'event qr code'; that head term belongs to /event-qr-code.

tools.name: Tickets & invites
tools.text: Link the ticket, the registration page or the calendar invite from a badge, poster or email.

tools.name: Venue WiFi
tools.text: Attendees scan to join the network — no password read out from the stage.

tools.name: Program & handouts
tools.text: One code on the signage opens the schedule, floor plan or slide deck as a PDF.

scenarios.t: A vCard code on the badge lets two attendees swap details in one scan.
A code on the desk sign opens the check-in or ticket page while people queue.
Schedule, floor plan and WiFi on the boards people already stop to read.
End each talk on a code to the deck, the feedback form or the speaker's socials.
Print one code instead of reprinting the program every time a slot moves.

faq.q: Is it free to use for an event?
faq.a: Yes — no sign-up, no watermark, and the codes never expire. Print as many badges and signs as you need.

faq.q: Can I change the schedule after printing?
faq.a: Yes, if the code points to a schedule you control. Edit that page or replace the PDF at the same link and the printed code keeps working.

faq.q: Does it count scans or check people in?
faq.a: No. These are static codes — they open a link. Any check-in or ticket scanning happens in your ticketing system, not here.

faq.q: I only need one ticket code. Where do I start?
faq.a: Use the event QR code page — it is the single-code tool. This page is for organizers printing several different codes.

pages.title: QR Codes for Hotels — Free Generator, No Sign-Up

pages.intro: The folder in the room goes out of date the week it is printed. A QR code on the desk card does the same job and can be updated without a reprint. Here are the codes hotels get the most use from, each with a free tool to make it.

tool.note: Full generator embedded on the ICP page. Low-priority page — keep it short.

tools.name: Guest WiFi
tools.text: Guests scan to join at check-in or from the desk card — no password spelled out.

tools.name: Room compendium
tools.text: Link the hosted guest directory — checkout times, housekeeping, local tips.

tools.name: Restaurant menu
tools.text: Room service and breakfast menus guests can read on their own phone.

scenarios.t: WiFi and the compendium on one small card beats a ring binder.
A code on the counter hands over WiFi and house info in one scan.
Local recommendations on a page you can change without reprinting.
Menu codes on table tents, same as any restaurant.

faq.q: Is it free for a hotel?
faq.a: Yes — no sign-up and no expiry. Print a card for every room at no cost.

faq.q: Do I need a new code when the WiFi password changes?
faq.a: Yes. A WiFi code stores the network name and password, so a new password means a new code and a new card.

faq.q: Can I update the compendium without reprinting?
faq.a: Yes, if the code points to a page or PDF you control. Replace the content at the same link and the printed code keeps working.

secondaries: how big should a qr code be on a menu
pages.title: QR Codes for Menus — Restaurant Setup Guide
pages.meta: Where to put a menu code, how big to print it, and how to keep the link stable. Free generator, no sign-up, codes never expire.
pages.subhead: A practical guide for restaurants: placement, print size, laminating, and keeping one link for good.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste the link to your menu page or hosted PDF'. This page is the guide; the primary CTA points to /menu-qr-code as the tool.

pages.intro: Getting a menu code right is mostly physical: where it sits on the table, how big it prints, and whether the link behind it survives your next website change. This guide covers table tents versus window decals, minimum print size, laminating without killing contrast, and how to pick a link you will never have to swap. Make the code itself on the menu tool, free and with no account.

howto.title: How to set up menu codes in a restaurant

steps.name: Pick a link you can keep
steps.text: Use one page you control and edit the menu behind it, so the printed code never changes.

steps.name: Print it big enough
steps.text: At least 3 cm on table tents and 8 cm on a window, with clear space all around.

steps.name: Place and test it
steps.text: Put it where a seated guest can reach, then scan it from that seat in the room's real lighting.

benefits.t: Tents get scanned at the seat; window codes only work for passers-by reading the menu outside.
Gloss throws overhead light straight back at the camera and kills the scan.
Point at a page you own, not a temporary share link, and you never reprint the code.
No account, no expiry — make a new tent for every seasonal menu at no cost.

faq.q: How big should the code be on a table tent?
faq.a: About 3 cm (1.2 in) across is the safe minimum for arm's-length scanning. Go to 8 cm or more for a window, which people scan from the pavement.

faq.q: Table tent or window sticker?
faq.a: Do both if you can. The tent serves seated guests; the window version lets people check the menu before deciding to come in.

faq.q: Will laminating stop it scanning?
faq.a: Matte laminate is fine and protects the card from spills. Avoid high-gloss finishes, which reflect ceiling lights into the camera.

faq.q: What if my menu link changes?
faq.a: The printed code is fixed, so choose a link you control and edit the menu behind it. Replacing the PDF at the same address keeps every printed code working.

secondaries: qr code for social media profiles
pages.title: QR Codes for Social Media — Free Generator
pages.meta: Turn any profile or link-in-bio into a scannable code. One code per platform or one for all. Free, no sign-up, download PNG or SVG.
pages.subhead: Send people straight to your profile from print — one code per platform, or one code for your whole link-in-bio.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste your profile or link-in-bio URL'. Hub page — surface links down to the Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and Spotify pages as the main next step.

pages.intro: There are two ways to do this. Give each platform its own code when the printed piece has room and you want people on one specific profile, or print a single code that opens your link-in-bio page listing everything. Either way it is a link, made in your browser, free, with no account and no expiry. Pick your platform below for a page with the exact profile-link format.

howto.title: How to make a social media QR code

steps.name: Decide one code or many
steps.text: One per platform for a specific follow, or one link-in-bio code when space is tight.

steps.name: Paste the profile link
steps.text: Copy the profile URL from a browser, not the in-app share sheet, so it stays a plain link.

steps.name: Brand it and download
steps.text: Match your colors, add a logo, then export SVG for print or PNG for screens.

benefits.t: Best on packaging, posters, and shop windows where a single clear ask converts better.
Point at your link-in-bio page and list every profile behind a single square.
Flyers, stickers, receipts, van livery — the camera app opens it, no app to install.
No sign-up, no watermark, no expiry. Print once and it keeps working.

faq.q: Should I make one code per platform or a single code?
faq.a: Use one per platform when you want a specific follow and have the space. Use a single link-in-bio code when the printed piece is small or you want to cover everything at once.

faq.q: Which platforms are covered?
faq.a: There are dedicated pages for Instagram, Facebook, YouTube and Spotify, each with the right profile-link format. Any other platform works too — just paste its profile URL.

faq.q: Where do I find my profile link?
faq.a: Open your profile in a desktop browser and copy the address bar. In-app share links sometimes carry extra tracking parameters you do not need.

faq.q: Is it free?
faq.a: Yes. Unlimited codes, no account, no watermark, and they never expire.

pages.title: QR Codes on Packaging — Free, Print-Ready
pages.meta: Put a QR code on your product box or label: how-to videos, reorder links, recycling info. Free, print-ready SVG, no sign-up, never expires.
pages.subhead: Ingredients, how-to videos, reorder links, recycling info — the box only holds so much text. Free and print-ready.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste the product page, video, or reorder link'. Emphasize SVG download for the print file and the quiet-zone and contrast notes.

pages.intro: A box has very little room, so the code carries the rest: full ingredients, a how-to-use video, a reorder link, or recycling instructions in every language you sell in. It is a link, not a file, so keep it pointed at a page you control. Export SVG and hand it to your printer with the rest of the artwork. Free, no account, no expiry.

howto.title: How to put a QR code on packaging

steps.name: Pick one job for the code
steps.text: Product info, a how-to video, a reorder link, or recycling details — one clear ask per code.

steps.name: Build in contrast and space
steps.text: Dark code on a light background, and leave a clear margin of at least four modules on every side.

steps.name: Export SVG for the print file
steps.text: Vector keeps the edges sharp at any size and drops straight into the artwork.

benefits.h: More than the box can hold
benefits.t: Full ingredients, manuals, and multi-language instructions live on the page behind the code.
Point at the product page so a used-up item is one scan away from being replaced.
SVG plus a proper quiet zone keeps the code readable at small label sizes.
No licence fee and no expiry, so a code printed on a two-year run keeps scanning.

faq.q: How small can a code be on a label?
faq.a: Keep it about 2 cm (0.8 in) across as a floor, and larger on anything a shopper reads at arm's length. Small print runs and coated stock both eat into readability.

faq.q: Does it still scan on a curved bottle or a pouch?
faq.a: Yes, if it is bigger and flatter than you think. Curves and creases distort the grid, so size up, keep the code off the sharpest part of the curve, and test a real sample.

faq.q: Can I use my brand colors?
faq.a: Yes, as long as the code stays clearly darker than its background. Light-on-dark and low-contrast pairings are the most common reason a printed code fails.

faq.q: Can I change where it points after printing?
faq.a: No — the code is a fixed link. Point it at a page you control so you can change the content behind it without reprinting.

pages.title: QR Codes for Feedback Forms — Free Generator
pages.meta: Make a QR code that opens your feedback form. Google Forms, Typeform, or your own survey. Free, no sign-up, download PNG or SVG.
pages.subhead: Point a code at your form and collect comments from the room, the table, or the shop floor. Free and no sign-up.

tool.note: URL mode, helper: 'paste the link to your feedback form or survey'. Cross-link to /google-review-qr-code for public review intent, which this page does not cover.

pages.intro: This is feedback that comes back to you: a Google Form, a Typeform, a survey tool, or an internal ticket form. Paste the form link, print the code, and put it where the experience happens — the table, the counter, the exit, the staff room. If you want public star ratings on your listing instead, the Google review page covers that. Free, no account, no expiry.

howto.title: How to make a feedback QR code

steps.name: Build the form first
steps.text: Keep it to two or three questions — long forms get abandoned on a phone.

steps.name: Paste the share link
steps.text: Copy the form's public link and check it opens on a phone before you print anything.

steps.name: Print and place it
steps.text: Download SVG and put it where the experience ends: the table, the receipt, or the exit.

benefits.t: Answers land in your form, not on a public listing, so you can fix things quietly.
Google Forms, Typeform, Microsoft Forms, or your own page — it is just a link.
A code at the table or the exit gets answers while the visit is still fresh.
Make as many as you need for different rooms, sites, or shifts. Codes never expire.

faq.q: How is this different from a Google review code?
faq.a: A review code sends people to your public Google listing. This one opens a private form you own, which suits internal, staff, and event feedback where you do not want the answers published.

faq.q: Which form tools work?
faq.a: Any that give you a public link — Google Forms, Typeform, Microsoft Forms, SurveyMonkey, or a form on your own site.

faq.q: Can I tell which location a response came from?
faq.a: Make a separate code per location, each pointing at its own form or a form link with the location pre-filled. The generator does not track scans.

faq.q: Where should I put it?
faq.a: Where the experience ends: the check presenter, the counter, the exit door, the last slide of a talk, or the back of a delivery note.

pages.title: About QR Code Agent — Free, Private QR Codes
pages.meta: Who makes QR Code Agent, why it is free, and how it stays that way. Every code is generated in your browser — nothing is ever uploaded.
pages.subhead: A free QR code generator that does not want your email address.

p: QR Code Agent makes QR codes for links, WiFi networks, contact cards, menus, PDFs and social profiles. You can style the dots and finder patterns, set your own colors, drop a logo in the middle, and download a PNG or a print-ready SVG.
There is no account, no watermark, no trial, and no expiry date. You do not give an email address, and there is no upsell waiting three clicks in.

Every code here is static. That means the destination is encoded directly into the pattern of the image — the code is the link, rather than a pointer to a redirect service that someone has to keep paying for.

This is the important difference between this tool and most "free" QR generators. A dynamic code routes through a third-party server, so it stops working the moment that service shuts down, changes its pricing, or decides your free trial is over. A static code has no such dependency. Print it once and it works for as long as the page it points to exists.

The trade-off is real and worth stating plainly: because the link is baked in, you cannot edit where a static code points after you download it. If the destination might change, point the code at a page you control and change the content on that page instead.

The generator runs entirely in your browser. The text you type, the WiFi password you enter, the contact details, and any logo you add are processed on your own device and never sent to a server. There is no copy of your code on our end, because there is no server involved in making it.
That is also why there are no scan counts or analytics dashboards for your codes: a static code does not report back to anyone, including us.
There is no cost to serve a static site, so there is no pressure to monetise you. There are no ads, no affiliate links, and nothing is sold.
If the tool saves you time, there is an optional coffee link in the header and footer. It is genuinely optional and nothing is gated behind it.
Built by Myk Pono. If something is broken or a QR type is missing, the fastest way to reach me is LinkedIn — the link is in the footer.

faq.q: Is it really free?
faq.a: Yes. No account, no watermark, no expiry, and no limit on how many codes you make.

faq.q: Can I use the codes commercially?
faq.a: Yes. The codes you generate are yours — use them on packaging, signage, menus, business cards, or anything else, including commercial work.

faq.q: Can I edit a code after downloading it?
faq.a: No. These are static codes, so the destination is fixed once downloaded. Point the code at a page you control if the destination may change.

pages.title: Privacy Policy — QR Code Agent
pages.meta: What QR Code Agent collects and what it does not. Your QR codes and logos never leave your browser. Cookieless analytics; GA4 only if you allow it.
pages.subhead: The short version: your QR codes never leave your browser, and we do not want your personal data.

sections.h: Your QR codes never leave your browser
p: Everything you enter into the generator — links, WiFi network names and passwords, contact details, and any logo image you add — is processed locally on your device by JavaScript running in your browser. None of it is transmitted to us or to anyone else, and none of it is stored on a server.
We therefore cannot see, recover, or share the contents of any code you make. Because the codes are static, they also do not report scans back to us or to you.
We use two analytics tools, and they are treated differently.
list: Umami — privacy-focused and cookieless. It records aggregate page views, the referring site, and coarse signals such as country, browser, operating system and device type. It sets no cookies, does not fingerprint you, and does not track you across other websites. It runs without asking, because it does not collect personal data.

Google Analytics 4 — cookie-based, and it does not load at all unless you press Allow on the consent banner. If you decline, or simply ignore the banner, the Google script is never fetched and no Google cookies are set. If you do allow it, IP anonymisation is enabled.

sections.h: What is stored on your device
p: A small amount of data is kept in your browser's local storage. It stays on your device, is never sent anywhere, and you can clear it at any time through your browser's site-data settings.
list: qra:consent — whether you allowed or declined Google Analytics, so you are not asked on every page.
qra:saved — any QR designs you choose to save, kept only so the saved-designs list works. Saved designs are local to that one browser and are not synced or backed up.

p: The optional coffee link sends you to a Stripe-hosted payment page. Payment is handled entirely by Stripe under their own privacy policy — we never see or receive your card details, and no payment information passes through this site.
Following that link is entirely voluntary, and nothing on the site is gated behind it.
These are the only external services this site contacts:
list: Umami (cloud.umami.is) — cookieless analytics, loaded on every page.
Google Analytics (googletagmanager.com) — loaded only after you press Allow.
Stripe (buy.stripe.com) — only if you click the support link.
Fonts are self-hosted on this domain, so no font provider sees your visit.

p: To withdraw analytics consent, clear this site's data in your browser settings. The banner will appear again on your next visit and you can decline.
Clearing site data also removes any saved QR designs, since those live only in your browser.
Questions about this policy can go to Myk Pono via the LinkedIn link in the footer. This page will be updated if the tools listed above change.
